text:
KINGSTON, Jamaica— Jamaica’s only hope of qualifying for the men’s 400m final at the Olympic Games, JeVaughn Powell came up short after he finished fourth in his semi-final heat at Stade de France in Paris on Tuesday.

Powell, who had a long season after competing in the American NCAA season, ran 44.91 seconds behind an impressive run from Great Britain’s Matthew Hudson-Smith, the World Championships silver medallist.

Michael Norman of the USA was second in 44.26 seconds and Nigeria’s Samuel Ogazi who ran a lifetime best 44.41 seconds to take one of the spots in the final.

Grenada’s Kirani James, who is seeking a fourth Olympic medal, led the qualifying with 43.78 seconds.

-Paul A Reid
